title: Entering the Promised Land
objective: Understand how God brought Israel into the land He promised to Abraham.

sections.content: After many years in the wilderness, God led Israel into the land promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

sections.text: Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the Lord your God is with you wherever you go.

sections.heading: God Keeps His Covenant
sections.content: God faithfully fulfilled His promise by bringing His people into the promised land.
God always keeps the promises He makes to His people.

questions: What promise did God fulfill by bringing Israel into the land?
Why was courage needed?
How does God guide His people today?

sections.content: Faithful Lord, help us trust Your promises and follow where You lead. Amen.

title: The Judges of Israel
objective: Learn about the leaders God raised up to guide Israel during troubled times.

sections.content: After settling in the land, Israel often turned away from God. During these times God raised up judges to rescue and guide His people.

sections.text: Whenever the Lord raised up judges for them, the Lord was with the judge.

sections.heading: Cycles of Sin and Rescue
sections.content: The book of Judges shows a repeating cycle: Israel sins, suffers oppression, cries out to God, and God sends a deliverer.
God is patient and merciful even when His people fail.

questions: What pattern appears repeatedly in Judges?
Why did Israel need judges?
What does this show about God's mercy?

sections.content: Merciful God, forgive our failures and guide us back to You. Amen.

title: Ruth and God's Faithfulness
objective: See how God worked through ordinary lives to preserve His promises.

sections.content: The story of Ruth shows God's faithfulness through loyalty, kindness, and redemption.

sections.text: Where you go I will go, and where you lodge I will lodge.

sections.heading: Faithfulness and Redemption
sections.content: Through Ruth and Boaz, God preserved the family line that would later lead to King David and ultimately to Jesus.
God works through everyday people to accomplish His purposes.

questions: Why is Ruth's loyalty significant?
How does this story connect to King David?
What does it teach about God's providence?

sections.content: Lord, help us live faithfully and trust Your plan. Amen.

title: Israel Wants a King
objective: Understand why Israel asked for a king and how God responded.

sections.content: Eventually the people of Israel asked for a king like the surrounding nations.

sections.text: They have not rejected you, but they have rejected me from being king over them.

sections.heading: Rejecting God's Kingship
sections.content: Israel desired a human king instead of trusting God fully as their ruler.
God alone is the true King of His people.

questions: Why did Israel want a king?
How did God respond?
What does this teach about trusting God?

sections.content: Lord, help us trust You as the true ruler of our lives. Amen.

title: King Saul
objective: Learn about Israel's first king and the consequences of disobedience.

sections.content: Saul became Israel's first king, but his disobedience eventually led to his downfall.

sections.text: To obey is better than sacrifice.

sections.heading: The Danger of Disobedience
sections.content: Saul's story reminds us that leadership without obedience to God leads to failure.
God calls His people to trust and obey His Word.

questions: Why did Saul lose God's favor?
What lesson does his story teach?
Why is obedience important?

sections.content: Lord, help us obey Your Word faithfully. Amen.

title: David and Goliath
objective: Understand how God used David to defeat Goliath.

sections.content: Young David trusted God to defeat the giant Goliath when no one else would.

sections.text: I come to you in the name of the Lord of hosts.

sections.heading: Trust in God's Strength
sections.content: David's victory reminds us that true strength comes from trusting the Lord.
God gives victory according to His will.

questions: Why was David confident?
What does this story teach about faith?
How can we trust God in difficult situations?

sections.content: Lord, help us trust Your strength instead of our own. Amen.

title: King David
objective: Learn about David's reign and God's covenant with him.

sections.content: David became Israel's greatest king and received a special promise from God.

sections.text: Your house and your kingdom shall be made sure forever before me.

sections.heading: The Davidic Covenant
sections.content: God promised that one of David's descendants would reign forever. This promise points to Jesus.
Jesus is the eternal King from the line of David.

questions: What promise did God make to David?
How does it point to Jesus?
Why is this covenant important?

sections.content: King Jesus, reign in our hearts forever. Amen.

title: King Solomon and the Temple
objective: Understand the building of the Temple and Israel's worship.

sections.content: Solomon built a magnificent temple in Jerusalem as a place for Israel to worship God.

sections.text: The glory of the Lord filled the house of the Lord.

sections.heading: Worship in the Temple
sections.content: The Temple symbolized God's presence with His people and the central place of worship.
God desires His people to worship Him faithfully.

questions: Why was the Temple important?
What did it represent?
How do Christians worship God today?

sections.content: Lord, receive our worship and praise. Amen.
